questions.question: Explain the difference between ∈ and ⊆ with examples.
expectedPoints: ∈ denotes element membership (a ∈ A means a is an element of A)
⊆ denotes subset relationship (A ⊆ B means all elements of A are in B)
A set can be an element of another set: {1} ∈ {{1}, {2}}

questions.question: Prove that A ∩ (B ∪ C) = (A ∩ B) ∪ (A ∩ C) using the element method.
expectedPoints: Show that A ∩ (B ∪ C) ⊆ (A ∩ B) ∪ (A ∩ C)
Take x ∈ A ∩ (B ∪ C), so x ∈ A and x ∈ B ∪ C
If x ∈ B, then x ∈ A ∩ B
If x ∈ C, then x ∈ A ∩ C
Thus x ∈ (A ∩ B) ∪ (A ∩ C)
Show that (A ∩ B) ∪ (A ∩ C) ⊆ A ∩ (B ∪ C)
Take x ∈ (A ∩ B) ∪ (A ∩ C)
If x ∈ A ∩ B, then x ∈ A and x ∈ B, so x ∈ B ∪ C
If x ∈ A ∩ C, then x ∈ A and x ∈ C, so x ∈ B ∪ C
Thus x ∈ A ∩ (B ∪ C)
Therefore, the sets are equal

questions.question: Given A = {1, 2, 3, 4}, B = {3, 4, 5, 6}, and C = {5, 6, 7, 8}, find A ∪ B ∪ C, A ∩ B ∩ C, and (A - B) ∪ (B - C).
expectedPoints: A ∩ B ∩ C = ∅ (no common element in all three sets)

questions.question: Explain the concept of power set and calculate P({a, b, c}).
expectedPoints: Power set is the set of all subsets of a given set
For set S with n elements, power set has 2ⁿ elements
Cardinality is 8

questions.question: Using the inclusion-exclusion principle, solve: In a class of 50 students, 30 play basketball, 25 play tennis, and 10 play both. How many play neither sport?
expectedPoints: Let B be basketball players, T be tennis players
Students who play at least one sport: 45
Total students: 50
Students who play neither: 50 - 45 = 5
Venn diagram verification
